VerbalDescription: erase(turn black) all shapes except the most common shape. leave those how they are.
Start by copying from input. You should only leave 2 matching shapes on the grid, they can be different colours but black out any shapes that do not match this criteria.

Resize your grid so it is the same as the original. Identify shapes that are the same as one another (i.e., there is 2 6x squares) and copy these over. Any shapes that are unique (i.e., don't have a twin(s)) do not replicate these.
find the shapes that have duplicates. keep those shapes in the same spots and with the same colors. clear all the other ones
keep output grid same as input. remove all squares that are part of unique shapes
Copy input, keep the shapes that are the same, delete every other shape

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You be shown several shapes, but you only need to copy the shapes which have been replicated more than once, regardless of the colour, as long as the shapes look the same, these one s should be copied over. The position and the colour of these shapes should not changed.
Using the same size grid, Copy the shapes that are the same patern and size onto the new grid keeping the positions and colours the same as original.
Reshape the grid to the target image. Find the shapes that match in the target image and place them where they are in the new grid, removing the shapes that don't match. Keep the colors the same.
Make the grid the same size as the original grid and create the middle most shapes with the same colors as the orginals.
Keep the same grid size. Find the objects that have the same shape even if they have different colors. Copy only these objects not changing their position or color.

Using the same grid size, replicate the 1st image exactly (with their same colors) and then remove every shape that there is only one of. (eg. if there are two triangles keep those regardless of color, if only one square remove that)
set grid to 8 x 8 and copy the positions and colours of the red and blue objects exactly.
The examples showed that the objects/ patterns of the same shape remained, irrespective of the colour. All other shapes were deleted.
Keep grid size the same. Keep all colours and shapes the same in the same place as orignal.
Copy grid size, shapes and colours, keep the shapes that match, remove the odd ones out

The rule is that you should remove anything that is not a regular shape and forms a group of two are more identical shapes. Copy the input grid uding the copy inout button. Then you should just press submit, as the shapes are regular and identical in shape.

Use the copy from input button.
Identify shapes that repeat regardless of colour. All other shapes that do not repeat replace with background colour.

Use the same grid size as the input. There will be a double design in the input, each design separated by a grey line. If the design is the same on the right and left sides of the grey line, duplicate the input in your answer without changing it. If the design is different on one side of the grey line, duplicate only the grey line and the design on the left, leaving the area on the right side of the line black.
The solution is to replicate the input completely

Copy out the grid exactly as you see it in the example, including colours and size. Your grid will be split into two halves by a coloured line through the middle. If the two halves of your grid look exactly the same, then leave your grid exactly as it is, so the it is the same as the example. If the right and left halves of your grid are different, then keep the left half the same, but set all the squares in the right half to black
If the two patterns seen are the same, copy exactly as it is from input. If they are different, again copy the input BUT after copying make the right side completely empty (black).
Click copy from input, now submit. That is all you do for this demonstration.
Copy from input. Remove any objects from right of grey line that do not match exactly what is left of grey line.

Enlarge the canvas to match the same size. Place a grey line in the middle. If both sides are the same, recreate them. If they are different, keep the left side only and recreate exactly with the same color and spacing.

Resize the grid to be the same size. Draw the grey line in the middle. On the original look at both sides of the grey line. If the objects are identical on both sides of the grey line then duplicate the picture in you new grid. If the objects are different on both side of the grey line then copy the left side of the grey line and leave the right side of the grey line empty.
If the shapes on either side are not identical, using black squares, remove the shape on the right using black squares
f they don't match then get rid of the pattern on the right
Keep only the shapes that are exactly in the same place in the second picture.

Copy the input. If the objects in the area to the right of the gray vertical dividing bar differ from the objects to the left of that bar in any way, erase those objects by coloring those tiles in with the color black.

There will be a gray line on the grid that will act as a divider on this. The Gray line remains as is. If on the left and right side there are exact duplicate shapes then they remain on the grid. If the shapes deviate in any form or fashion on the right side, be it shape size or color, then the objects on the right are removed.

make a grid the same size as the original grid. draw a gray line down the center of the grid. If the original shape to the left of the gray line matches the shape to the right of the gray line, then exactly copy both sides to the new grid. If the shape on the right hand side of the gray line does not match the shape on the left, then only copy the left hand shape to the new grid and leave the side to the right of the gray line blank
Select correct number of squares. Draw five objects. Copy objects exactly.
Copy exactly what was shown to you. The size of the grid and the colors and placement.
Copy only the objects that come in pairs by shape.
Eliminate the odd shape out.
matching shapes remain witin the grid, same size, same colour, unique shapes are removed
Copy from the demo. Remove the object on the right.
delete the block closest to the edges of the space.
Ensure the black grid is the same size as the input.  Delete one or more shapes entirely- if there are 3 shapes delete 1, if there are 6 delete 2.
Keep only the shapes that have multiples in the image
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be duplicate objects, simply remove the object(s) that do not have a duplicate or do not match.
there has to be 2 of the same shape in the output. so if there is only 1 of a certain shape, delete it
Duplicate the shapes and grid and then remove shapes which do not have a matching pair (shape only - may be mixed colour)
The grid alligned and was eliminated
Copy from input; Remove everything that doesn't have a duplicate of itself.
You are getting rid of anything that doesn't have a matching shape. So in the end, there should be two solid squares and two large squares with tiny squares in the middle.

Use the same grid size as the test grid. Using the same colors as the test grid, remake any objects that are the same shape as another object in the test grid. Objects that are a different shape from all other objects in the test grid should be left out.

The grid size, colors, and locations all remain the same. Determine if any of the colored shapes are the same exact shape (color doesn't matter). Any shape that doesn't have a pair gets deleted/blacked out.
Copy the grid exactly.  Copy only the shapes that have matching shapes.  Use the same colors.

Use Copy from input button 
make black any shape that does not repeat, keeping only the repeating shapes
Remove any object that is the only color of it's kind. If there are multiple objects in the same color, draw those objects using the same color, grid size, and position.

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be multiple objects. Some of these objects are going to have the same color that create pairs, and some objects are not. Simply, remove the objects that do not have the same color that cannot create a pair. (Shapes do not matter)
Copy grid from input and black out any objects with a non-duplicating color.

Use the same grid size as input. You should see multiple shapes - where there is more than one shape of the same colour, you should copy these shapes across to the grid exactly (same position, shape, orientation, size, and colour). You should ignore and NOT copy any shape where its colour does not feature in another shape.
Grid size should be the same. Keep only the two shapes which match in colour. Black out the shapes that are odd in colour.

Look at the input grid, there will be several shapes in there, take note of the colour of each shape. The answer is the same grid size as the input but the shapes you have to draw are the ones where there is more than one shape in the same colour. Copy the shapes in size, location and colour of each shape where there is more than one of that colour.

Keep the grid size the same. Copy over objects that have a matching color with another object in the grid. Keep the positions the same as they originally are. Ignore any other objects that do not have a match.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Copy the object from the input. Remove any objects that are not the same color as a another object.
Copy the exact grid size. Identify any shapes that share the same color and copy those over to the new grid exactly. Disregard any shapes that are only present in a color once.

Use the same grid size as in the input.  There will be two objects in the input.  Use the two objects that are the same color and do not shift them. Remove the other objects that do not have a matching color.
Recreate the picture but omit any shapes whose colour is only represented once. So recreate the picture but only recreate shapes whose colour is represented more than once.
keep output grid same as input. remove all colours that have only one instance
Find the color that has the most shapes then get rid of the rest of the shapes that are not that color. If there is a tie then get rid of the ones that are not tied.
Copy input.  Then erase any objects that only have one object of a color.  If it has two objects of the same color, keep them.
Eliminate all squares that don't belong to a majority group, based on the number of individual solid objects depicted.
Expand the grid to match the sample.  Copy the shapes that are duplicates, match shape, color, and location.
Using the same size grid. Copy the Shapes that have another shape of the same color (pink and orange). Remove the other shapes (green and yellow).
Have the same grid size. Simply copy across any shape that was orange or yellow.

Set grid size to 12 by 12. Identify objects that share a color with another object. Move those objects into their same locations on the 12 by 12 grid. Do not transfer objects that do not share a color.
Copy from input. Now remove the colors that don’t have a pair. So for this one, remove the yellow line and the green line. Make those cells black. Now submit.
Click copy from input. Now remove the yellow line and green line, change those cells to black.
Use the same size grid as the input. There will be many lines in the input. Identify which lines are parallel, replicate these two lines and delete the others
Keep just the two lines that are parallel to each other.

Change the grid size to the size of the task (10x10) then copy the whole task over with our making any changes to colour or the position of the shapes.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Keep the 2 lines that are going the same way and remove the others. keep it's color the same

The goal is to remove everything EXCEPT for a pair of parallel lines. Because the test only contains one set of parallel lines, no changes are required. Just keep the grid size at 10x10 and copy the shapes as they appear in the test
Set grid to 10 by 10. Transfer objects over to new grid. Use the same color.
Do not change anything
Find the 2 objects that have the same shape. Remove the other objects from the grid.
Resize grid to 10x10. The test image should contain a single set of parallel lines. Identify them and recreate them in the target grid. Do not make any changes or add any other shapes.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Select copy from input. Select the objects between the left and the right object and make them black squares to remove them.
just guessed, no explanation really.

Recreate the canvas. You will see multiple shapes on the canvas. See which direction MOST of the shapes go (horizontal, vertical, diagonal). Whichever of those 3 has the most pick the shapes that go in that direction and remake them exactly. The rest go black.
Resize your grid to exactly one cell, fill the cell with color of the center of provided shapes whichever has more, example, you got 2 orange inners and 1 blue, then fill your target with Orange
Resize the grid to 1 by 1. Look at the color of the center part of all the objects in the original grid. Now color our 1 by 1 grid to the color that the majority of those centers are.
Determine which color fill is most used in the picture and make a 1x1 grid of that color
See which colour is represented in the middle of more than one shape. Whatever colour this is make a grid of one by one square in this colour.

Check the input grid and the shapes that there are. Take note of the colours that appear in the centre of the shapes. The colour that appears the most frequently in the centre is the colour you will need for your answer. Create a 1 by 1 grid and colour it to match the colour that appears the most frequently in the middle of the shapes in the input.
resize the grid to one square,color the square to match the color in the middle of the square on the upper left hand size
Look at the center color of your objects.  Whatever color appears most often that is the color of your new one by one grid.

There is 4 boxes all with a different colored square in the middle from the perimeter. In 2/4 of these boxes the middle square is the same color whereas the other 2 are different colors from each other. The grid simply becomes a single block of that middle color with 2/4 in them.
The grid is 1x1 and the color will be whatever color that is seen most inside the square of the original image.
Make a 1 block grid the color of the interior block of upper left side design
change th grid to a singe 1x1 square. fill that square with the central colour of the the coloured object in the top leftmost position
check the center color of each shape, choose the one which appears most. select same height and width as demonstration and fill in the colour
which suqare high

Make the grid one row by one column and fill it with the color of the block in the middle of the left top object.

In the input, there will be multiple colored outlines.  In the middle of those outlines there could be either a single block or a small shape with multiple blocks.  The middle shapes will have multiple colors.  Identify the majority color of the middle shapes.  Make a single block of that majority color as the output.

Change the size of the grid to 1 x 1. you only want one square to be visible on the grid. Each shape on the example has an inner colour. choose which colour appears most times in the shape. make the one square on the grid this colour. submit.
Resize the grid to 1x1. Look at the original grid and determine which colour appears the most inside the original shapes. Change the 1x1 grid to that colour.
Look at the internal colours of the shapes. Two shapes have the same colour inside. Make a 1x1 grid in that colour
Keep the same grid size and keep the shapes in the same location with the same colors, but eliminate all shapes that have an identical counterpart. Consider colors inside another color as one shape.
Use the same grid size as the input. Copy the objects but remove the pair that has the same colors.

Make the grid size the same as the input. Copy the two shapes across that are not the same colour exactly as on the input grid. The two shapes that are the same colour should not be copied, the space should now be black.

use same grid size as input, there was 4 shapes, 2 are the same colour and the other 2 are different, the final should just be the 2 shapes that are different colours on the same size grid and location
Copy grid and remove the two patterns which are the same as each other

Same grid size. You may see four shapes. They will be different colours and shapes except two of the shapes will be the same colour. Keep the image exactly the same except remove the shape(s) that are the same colour. Quite a simple one!
Two objects with same colours removed

count the squares left to right, top to bottom eg 12 by 12

see which demo has the same colour and which one does not.

only create the diagram if it has different colour. For example, red and blue will need to be created as they are two differnet colour. If it was red and red, you will not need to create the diagram

Note which objects in the input contain the same color combination. Create a grid the same size as the input. Add only those objects that don't share the same combination of colors. Put the remaining objects in the output in their same positions and colors.
Remove any objects that are of the exact same colour combination.
Copy the input grid. Delete all objects whose color patterns are the same, leaving only objects that contrast in color.
Delete the two shapes that are the same color scheme. Leave the grid the same size and then move all of the other shapes left to the new grid.

The input should be copied, with the grid size staying the same. Each object that is its own color scheme should be kept as is, but if there are two shapes with the same exterior and interior colors, those should be deleted and filled with black squares.
Resize the grid as seen in the input. Copy all shapes except for the ones that have the same colours.
Remove any objects that consist of the exact same colour combination. Regardless of their shape.

Identify the shapes within other shapes. There should be two which are the same shapes but might be different colours. Copy these matching shapes to the answer (same position and colour as in original).

First, use the same size grid as the input. Then only copy what is inside the shape on the top left that you see. Fill in the green squares at exactly the same place they are on the input. Then also do the same for the shape that is diagonal to it remembering only to fill in what is inside the shape and not the colours on the outside of it.

Resize the grid to 15x15. There are smaller shapes within the bigger shapes, you need to copy the two smaller shapes which are the same size (They're 2 x 2 size squares - one blue and one green) Copy those to the exact same positions as the image is showing.
choose the most common shape from the input grid and remove all other colours in the output grid
the rule was to pick out the shape that was formed the most and draw those shapes on a black grid of the same size as the test grid.

For this task the rule was to pick out the most commonly formed shape and recreate them on the user solution grid. the most common shape on the frist task demonstration is the plus symbols. the second, the "C" shape and the third, the 2x3 squares.
The rule for this one was to determine which shape was most commonly formed within the grid. the task was to output the most common shapes in their original colours ONLY.
Copy over the two matching internal shapes only.
Copy grid.  Delete anything except the interior shape in the top left and the corresponding interior shape found in the remaining groups.
Keep the grid the same size. There should be two small shapes within larger shapes that match in size and shape. Put those on the new grid in their original colors and locations.

Okay you should see for sets of shapes within shapes.  You'll need to identify the two shapes that are the same in the inner set.  Once you figure this out your goal is to erase everything else but the two matching shapes keeping the original color of the shapes and keeping them in the same location.  So you'll have an all black grid and two identical shapes left but keeping the original color and location of the two shapes.

Use the same grid size as the input. In the input, you will see many shapes with shapes contained within them. Find the two inner shapes that are the same (two crosses, too rectangles), though they may be different colors. Reproduce only those two shapes and everything else should be black squares.
The input will have objects contained within other objects. Same grid size. Identify a matching pair of contained objects in the input. Replicate exactly only these.
leave the same shapes and remove all other cells
Resize the grid to match the original. Decide which two inner shapes match. Recreate those in shape, location, and color on the blank grid.

Use the same grid size as the test grid size. There are multiple objects with different color patterns inside them. Remake any patterns that are the same shape as another pattern in the test grid, using the same colors and locations as the test grid. Do not remake the objects surrounding the patterns.
Find similar shapes and leave only the inner shapes on the grid. First size grid the same. Then recreate the inner two shapes that are similar on the board using the tools.

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be 4 objects in the input. Within each object will be an identifiable shape. Two of these shapes will be identical. Keep the color of these two shapes. Fill in all other squares black.
Copy input, delete everything except the two shapes that are the same.

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You be see a few different shapes, which are essentially made up of larger shapes with smaller shapes inside, which are of a different colour. You only need to copy the smaller interior shapes, which have been included more than once, you need to copy these over actually as they are, with the positioning and colour to match.

The input is divided into two areas of mostly solid colour. In each of these areas, there is an object. If these objects are different, return a 1x1 black grid. If they are identical, the output grid should be the object with a black background on a grid of minimally-necessary size to contain it.

Check to see if the shape on both sides of the test input grid are the same (do not take the background colour or size into account), if they are the same, change the grid size to match the width and height of the shape and redraw it in the exact same colour. If the shapes are not the same, then change the grid size to 1x1 and leave it black.
If the inside of the patterns matches their backgrounds, copy the shape and make the background black.  If the shapes are solid, the solution is a single black box

copy the shape into a grid the size of the shape, keeping the shape the same colour and the backgound of the shape is black. If the shape is a solid block with no background, just put 1x1 grid with a black square.
grid 3 x 4, leave empty

Well you'll see to large areas of different colors divided by a black line (it could be vertical or horizontal) and in each of those colored areas is a shape.  If the shapes are the same size and color you'll need to reshape the grid so the edges of the grid contain the shape. Now you may have edges uncolored which is okay as long as there's at least one colored square on each edge that's fine it just has to be the smallest grid possible to contain the shape. Any part that is not the shape should be colored in black. Again use the original color of the shape for the shape.

In the input, there are two sections divided by a black line. In each of these sections will be a shape. If the shape is the same in both sections then your answer will be to recreate that shape (same grid and colour). If the shapes are different in the two sections then the answer will be a single black square

If the shapes are the same on both sides of the black line then adjust the image to only fill the area of the shape and then copy the shape as shown (if they are identical). if shapes are not identical then do a 2x2 black square
Keep the grid size and position of the objects the same. If the two squares are the same size, copy them on the new grid in light blue. If the squares are different sizes, copy them in gray.

Recreate the grid as pictured, but only use either cool blue or grey for all squares. If the topmost object is smaller than the bottom most, use grey. If they're the same size or there is only one object, use cool blue.

Copy the input image exactly to the output.  There will be 2 outlines of squares.  If both outlines are the same size, make the outline light blue.  If they are different sizes, make the outlines grey.

Resize the grid to 10x10. There are two items in the input box. If the items are the same, then reproduce the items in the same locations at the same size, only changing the color of the items to light blue. If the items are different, then reproduce the items in the same locations at the same size, only changing the color of the items to gray.

Copy the input and the only thing you will change is the color of the shapes. If the shapes are the same size, change the color of the shapes to gray. If the shapes are not the same size, change the color of the shapes to light blue.

Keep the grid size and positions of the objects the same. The color of the objects changes depending on the sizes of the objects. If the objects are the same size then change the color of both to light blue. If the objects are not the same size, change the color of both to grey.

Copy the grid and the shapes. If the two squares are the same size then change the colour of the shapes to pale blue. If the two squares are different sizes then changes their colour to grey. The background remains black.
If shapes are same size, duplicate and recolour light blue (same positions). If shapes are different sizes, recolour grey (same positions)
Copy from input, if the yellow shape is below the orange shape, then make both shapes grey.
Reproduce the grid identically to the original, but make both shapes the same grey colour.

You will see two square shapes of coloured blocks. These squares can be different sizes, shapes and may be touching or overlapping. 
The solution is that is both square shapes are exactly the same size and shape. Then you should recreate the grid input exactly but colour both shapes light blue.
If the square shapes are not exactly the same in size and shape then colour them both grey.
Copy the input. If the items are the same size, change the color of both items to light blue. If they are different sizes, change the color of the items to light grey.
Copy the input. If the shapes are the same size, change their colors both to dark blue. If the shapes are different sizes, change their colors both to grey.
Adjust grid to example size. IF the two shapes are the same size make them blue. Otherwise, make the shapes grey. Do not move anything.

Copy out the same size grid as the example given. If the two shapes in the example are the same size, then trace the image and colour it light blue. If the shapes in the example are different sizes, colour it grey.

Copy the position and size of the shapes from the demonstration. If the two squares are the same size, change their color to light blue. If the two squares different sizes, change their color to gray.
